title: A cast shall not remove any const or volatile qualification from the type of a pointer or reference
description: A cast shall not remove any const or volatile qualification from the type of a pointer or referenceAnalyze your codeadaptability-modularmaintainabilityCode Smellcppcoreguidelinescertmisra-c++2008misra-c2004suspiciousmisra-c2012Why is this an issue?More InfoUsingconstin your code improves reliability and maintenance. When passing aconstvalue, developers assume that its value won’t be changed. But usingconst_cast<>()to cast away aconstqualifier, destroys developer assumptions and code reliability.
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

checks.message: Cast to unqualified pointer type detected. Review to ensure const/volatile qualifications are not improperly removed.
